judge_reason: Both descriptions agree on the core visual content—a photograph of an experimental aircraft with a large, dark, circular rotor on a strut above a fuselage, identified as Jonathan E. Caldwell's 'Gray Goose' helicopter—but Description B adds specific caption text and document metadata not present in Description A without contradicting the visual elements.
original_image_tags: A black-and-white newspaper photograph showing an experimental aircraft on the ground. The aircraft features a large, dark, circular rotor or disc mounted atop a strut above a conventional fuselage, wings, and a multi-wheeled landing gear. The setting appears to be an airfield with a fence and a vintage car visible in the background.
gpt_mini_desc: *Image: A newspaper clipping photograph of a helicopter-like aircraft with a large circular disk-shaped top and landing gear, printed with the caption “AIR FORCE FINDS ‘FLYING SAUCERS’—This is Jonathan E. Caldwell’s ‘Gray Goose’ helicopter pictured before it made a near-disastrous test flight of about a minute in Washington nearly 10 years ago.”*
